perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 1.1 In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words shall have the following meaning: "AFFILIATES" means any company, corporation, partnership, joint venture or other entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with XFN;
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 1.1 In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words shall have the following meaning: "AFFILIATES" means any company, corporation, partnership, joint venture or other entity that directly controls XFN, only and no other entity;
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'AFFILIATES' has been narrowed to include only entities that *directly* control XFN, excluding entities indirectly controlling or under common control with XFN. This contradicts the clause in 2.1 Exclusive Rights in the Territory section which grants rights to affiliates that are indirectly controlled by XFN, creating ambiguity about the extent of the license granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. GRANT OF RIGHTS 2.1 Exclusive Rights in the Territory: CEIS hereby grants XFN and its Affiliates an exclusive license (free of all third-party liens, claims and encumbrances) to, and to permit others to, during the Term in the Territory: Source: XINHUA SPORTS & ENTERTAINMENT LTD, F-1, 2/21/2007 (a) store or cache the Content in one or more host computers controlled directly or indirectly by XFN or its Affiliates; (b) adapt, translate, modify, reproduce, copy, amend, revise or encode the Content; and (c) publish, broadcast, distribute, re-distribute, transmit, display, make available to the public or otherwise exploit the Content in any manner by any device or in media now or hereafter known.
perturbation.justification: The change in the definition of 'AFFILIATES' significantly narrows its scope to only entities directly controlling XFN. This creates a direct contradiction with section 2.1, which grants rights to affiliates controlled 'directly or indirectly' by XFN. The modification removes 'indirectly' controlled entities from the definition, thus conflicting with the grant of rights to those same entities, creating an in-text contradiction about who can exercise the rights. This leads to ambiguity and impacts enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 3. DELIVERY OF CONTENT 3.1 During the Term of this Agreement, CEIS shall supply the Content of XFN by such means of delivery or transmission as may be reasonably required by XFN including by online transmission. 3.2 CEIS shall use its best endeavours to ensure that the Content is made available to XFN on a continuous, uninterrupted real-time basis.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. DELIVERY OF CONTENT 3.1 During the Term of this Agreement, CEIS shall supply the Content of XFN by a single delivery via secure FTP within 30 days of the Effective Date. No ongoing delivery is required. 3.2 CEIS is not obligated to make the Content available to XFN on a continuous, uninterrupted real-time basis.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to Clause 3 fundamentally alters the content delivery obligations. The original text mandates continuous, real-time delivery. The altered text limits delivery to a single instance, eliminating the continuous requirement. This change contradicts the definition of "CONTENT" in Clause 1.1, which refers to content published 'from time to time'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 1.1 In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words shall have the following meaning: "AFFILIATES" means any company, corporation, partnership, joint venture or other entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with XFN; "CONTENT" means real-time economic news including articles, reports, data, information and such materials that have or have been and/or will be published from time to time and that is or will be in the possession or control of CEIS from time to time, in respect of the subject matters as more particularly described in Schedule 1; EFFECTIVE DATE means 18 May 2000; "INTELLECTUAL means patents, trade marks, service marks, trade names, PROPERTY RIGHTS" design rights (whether registrable or not), any applications for the foregoing, copyright and other assignable intellectual property -1- rights (whether registrable or not) in any country, including but not limited to the format, layout, and the look and feel of any of the Content; "TERM" means the term as set out in Clause 7; and "TERRITORY" means the world excluding the People's Republic of China.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the delivery of 'CONTENT' from a continuous, real-time basis to a single delivery. This directly contradicts Clause 1.1, which defines 'CONTENT' as materials published 'from time to time'. The changed text eliminates the 'from time to time' aspect, creating a clear in-text contradiction according to the provided definition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. TERMINATION 8.1 XFN may terminate this Agreement by giving thirty (30) days written notice to the CEIS. 8.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement: (a) if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement which is not capable of being remedied; (b) if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement which is capable of being remedied but not remedied within thirty (30) days upon receiving written notice from the non-breaching party requiring remedy; and (c) if the other Party becomes insolvent or bankrupt. 8.3 Upon termination of the Agreement: (a) CEIS shall terminate the transmission of the Content with immediate effect; and (b) in the event that this Agreement is terminated prior to the expiry of the Term, XFN shall recover any sums paid to CEIS in advance for the unexpired Term of this Agreement, together with interest from the date those sums were paid until the date of full refund.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. TERMINATION 8.1 XFN may terminate this Agreement by giving thirty (30) days written notice to the CEIS. However, such termination will incur a penalty fee equivalent to 50% of the remaining contract value. 8.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement: (a) if the other Party commits a minor breach of this Agreement. (b) if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement which is capable of being remedied but not remedied within ninety (90) days upon receiving written notice from the non-breaching party requiring remedy; and (c) if the other Party is perceived to be insolvent. 8.3 Upon termination of the Agreement: (a) CEIS shall terminate the transmission of the Content within a reasonable timeframe; and (b) in the event that this Agreement is terminated prior to the expiry of the Term, XFN shall not recover any sums paid to CEIS in advance for the unexpired Term of this Agreement. Any pre-paid sums shall be forfeited as liquidated damages to CEIS.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces several contradictions within the Termination clause. Firstly, a penalty is now introduced for termination by XFN. Secondly, the threshold for termination based on breach is lowered to 'minor breach,' while the timeframe to remedy a material breach is extended to 90 days. Thirdly, the determination of insolvency is changed to 'perceived to be insolvent', removing the need to prove that. Finally, refunds upon termination are removed. This contradicts the concept of 'material breach' as used in the original contract, especially in section 8.2 and creates uncertainty about the rights and obligations upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement: (a) if the other Party commits a minor breach of this Agreement. (b) if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement which is capable of being remedied but not remedied within ninety (90) days upon receiving written notice from the non-breaching party requiring remedy; and (c) if the other Party is perceived to be insolvent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several significant in-text contradictions. Firstly, the introduction of a penalty fee for XFN's termination in 8.1 directly contradicts the original concept of termination at will with only thirty days notice. Secondly, the lowering of the breach threshold to a 'minor breach' in 8.2(a) and lengthening the timeframe to remedy a material breach to ninety days creates inconsistency with the prior agreement regarding material breach. Furthermore, changing the insolvency determination to 'perceived to be insolvent' introduces ambiguity and potential for misuse. Finally, the removal of refunds in 8.3(b) directly negates the prior agreement to refund sums for the unexpired term. As such, the changed text clearly leads to conflict, unenforceability and a legal issue.
